feed_role: Lab = generator/feed. The clinic SM pipeline (cockpit) owns GATE-1 dedupe, floor-locked rendering, the single preview, and publishing.
note: One creative reused across Instagram, Facebook & Google Business Profile. 10 posts to choose from, diversified by format/hook/topic, each modelled on a niche over-performer and rewritten in EHN clinician voice. Each post carries a routing block mapping it to the pipeline's pillar→format schedule.

pillar_format_map.conversion · Sun: text-led CTA carousel (booking/assessment link in the caption, not on a card)

palette.note: EHN palette only — never the competitor colours/fonts.

typography.note: NOT Libre Caslon — that is the branded-card lock, benched for this experiment.

render_constraints.rule: Shorten copy, never shrink font. The renderer hard-asserts >=36px and crashes the build on an under-floor size.
render_constraints.card_branding: Cards carry the business name "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green close line) — NOT the suburb. Suburb keyword lives in the GBP caption only (local SEO).
render_constraints.gbp: Every post ships a GBP hook card, including text-only days (Zernio silently schedules a card-less GBP post — always supply one).

posts.visual_recipe: Solid sage-green background (muted, professional). White serif headlines in large, bold font (18-24pt equiv.) centered top-left to center: "Why Calcium-D-Glucarate is a Game-Changer for Histamine Intolerance." Below, smaller white sans-serif body copy (12-14pt) in sentence case explaining the supplement. Small dark grey slide indicator (1/7) top right. Branded handle in white sans-serif at bottom. No images, faces, or overlays—pure text-on-colour design prioritizing legibility and authority.
posts.angle: Why oestrogen dominance and histamine intolerance so often show up together — and what functional medicine looks for to help.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: 1080px square carousel, 7 slides. Colour palette: cream (#F5F3F0) background as the dominant field; EHN green (#39B54A) as the primary visual anchor appearing on every slide in a vertical stripe or accent bar; secondary soft charcoal (#3D3D3D) for text hierarchy; white space used generously to match the winner's clinical, uncluttered feel.

Slide layout (all slides follow this structure):
• Background: soft cream
• Left or top accent: vertical stripe or bar in EHN green (#39B54A) — occupying ~15–20% of the slide width, or a top bar
• Headline: positioned top-left to center, Satoshi Bold 700, ~95–110px on 1080 canvas, charcoal (#3D3D3D), with ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic (e.g., "Oestrogen" in green)
• Body copy: Satoshi Medium 500, ~45–48px, charcoal, sentence case, positioned below headline with breathing room (no crowding)
• Slide indicator: small charcoal text "1/7" (or 2/7, etc.) top right, ~36px
• Brand lockup: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in charcoal + green, bottom right corner, unobtrusive, ~32px

Specific slide content (see on_image for exact wording):
- Slide 1: Hook headline + opening stat (e.g., oestrogen–histamine link). Green stripe on left, headline centred, body below.
- Slides 2–6: Each explores one functional-medicine angle (mast cells, DAO enzyme, oestrogen metabolism, liver detox, gut health, etc.). Same structure: headline + brief explanation.
- Slide 7: Closing call-to-action. "Ready to explore your hormone story?" + CTA button or text in green.

Visual tone: text-on-colour (no images, no stock photos, no faces), pure typography-driven design that reads as clinical, authority-backed, and calm. White space prioritised. Serif fonts NOT used; Satoshi only. Palette is EHN green anchored, with cream + soft charcoal as supporting cast — never the competitor's sage/white combination.
posts.design_recipe:
Reusable carousel template:

**Canvas:** 1080px square, 7-slide carousel.

**Colour palette (topic-agnostic):**
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (appears on every slide as a vertical stripe, top bar, or accent element)
- Background: cream #F5F3F0 (warm, clinical, non-clinical feel)
- Text hierarchy: charcoal #3D3D3D (headlines + body), white for contrast if needed
- Secondary accent: soft sage or muted blue-grey (optional, to complement green per topic mood)
- Never: pure black, pure white backgrounds, or the competitor's sage-green dominance

**Typography (all slides):**
- Headline: Satoshi Bold 700, ~95–110px equivalent, charcoal #3D3D3D, with ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic
- Body: Satoshi Medium 500, ~45–48px equivalent, charcoal, sentence case, short phrases (max 2–3 lines per slide)
- Slide indicator: ~36px, charcoal, top right
- Brand lockup: "Elemental Health & Nutrition", charcoal + green accent, bottom right, ~32px

**Layout structure (all slides):**
1. Vertical green stripe or accent bar (15–20% width or top bar placement) — the visual anchor
2. Headline positioned top-left to center (leaving top margin)
3. Body copy below headline with 30–40px breathing room
4. Slide indicator (top right)
5. Brand lockup (bottom right)

**Visual tone:** Text-on-colour only. No photography, faces, illustrations, or overlays. Pure typography-driven design. White space is generous and intentional. Professional, calm, grounded. Editorial (not wellness-cliché).

**Flex per topic:** Secondary colour (e.g., teal, warm cream accent) may shift per post topic, but green #39B54A is the immovable anchor on every slide, and the layout structure remains identical.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1: "Oestrogen dominance and histamine intolerance | Often show up together" | Supporting stat: "Excess oestrogen can trigger mast cells to release more histamine"

Slide 2: "The loop: oestrogen → mast cells | Release histamine"

Slide 3: "Histamine builds up | Because DAO enzyme slows down"

Slide 4: "Poor oestrogen metabolism | Liver can't clear excess efficiently"

Slide 5: "Gut dysbiosis makes it worse | Beta-glucuronidase breaks down oestrogen again"

Slide 6: "What we look for | Symptom patterns · Cycle tracking · Functional labs"

Slide 7: "Ready to explore your hormone story? | Book a consultation" | "Elemental Health & Nutrition"
posts.caption:
Oestrogen dominance and histamine intolerance are cousins — they feed each other in ways many practitioners miss.

Here's the functional-medicine lens: excess oestrogen can trigger mast cells to release histamine; at the same time, histamine slows the DAO enzyme that would normally break it down. Add in sluggish liver detox and gut dysbiosis, and you get a cycle that amplifies both.

We don't treat symptoms in isolation. We look at hormone metabolism, liver clearance, gut health, and your actual cycle patterns to understand what's driving it.

If you've felt stuck on standard treatments, or you suspect oestrogen is playing a bigger role than anyone's mentioned, let's talk.

Book a consultation and we'll map out your hormone story.

#oestrogenhealth #perimenopause #histamineintolerance #functionalhealth #hormonebalance
posts.gmb_caption: Oestrogen dominance and histamine intolerance often work together — and standard care can miss the connection. We use functional medicine to understand your unique hormone patterns and support your body's natural detox systems. If you're in Eastwood, Adelaide, and ready to explore what's driving your symptoms, book an appointment with us today.

posts.visual_recipe: Clean minimalist layout with light grey/white background. Small circular headshot (tan/beige) of verified doctor on left. Bold sans-serif black text centered right, opening with punchy 5-word hook ("Laughter is anti-inflammatory") followed by parallel structure list of 5 physiological claims. Concluding statement isolated below in larger, heavier font. No photo overlays or illustration—pure typography with single portrait anchor.
posts.angle: Laughter and joy are measurable nervous-system regulators — not optional extras.
posts.build_brief: Canvas: single square card (1080×1080px). Background: warm cream (#F5F1ED or similar soft neutral that complements EHN green). Layout replicates the winner's minimalist composition exactly: small circular headshot (80–100px) of a warm, approachable EHN clinician positioned on the LEFT side, roughly upper-third; headshot has a soft sage or muted green border (using a tint of #39B54A at ~30% opacity) to anchor the green. Main copy block sits CENTERED-RIGHT, with ample whitespace between headshot and text. Headline: "Laughter is regulatory" (8 words max) rendered in Satoshi Bold at ~95px, with the word "regulatory" in green #39B54A italic to create a single green anchor. Below headline, a short parallel-structure list (5 brief phrases, each ~40px Satoshi Medium) describing physiological outcomes, e.g. "Lowers cortisol | Shifts nervous system | Releases tension | Boosts immunity signalling | Restores vagal tone". Each list item sits on its own line, left-aligned to the text block, maintaining visual rhythm. Conclude with an isolated statement in heavier Satoshi Bold (~60–70px) positioned lower, set in charcoal, e.g. "Joy is biology." Bottom of card: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A (small, ~24px, Satoshi Medium) as the close line. Overall feel: clean, clinical, warm — typography-forward with a single human anchor and green as the primary visual signal. No illustrations, no photo overlays, no distracting elements. The card breathes: generous margins, clear hierarchy, and the soft cream background lets the green and charcoal speak.
posts.design_recipe: REUSABLE TEMPLATE: Minimalist stat-drop card for any functional-medicine topic (mental health, digestion, immunity, inflammation, sleep, etc.). Canvas: 1080×1080px square card. Background: warm cream or soft neutral (never pure white, never full green, chosen to harmonize with #39B54A). Layout: Small circular headshot (80–100px) of clinician/practitioner positioned on LEFT, upper-third, with soft sage/muted green border (~30% opacity of #39B54A) as the green anchor. Main text block sits CENTERED-RIGHT with ample whitespace margin between headshot and text block. Headline: Satoshi Bold ~95px, ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic (or complementary accent if context allows); headline ≤8 words. Supporting list: 5 short parallel-structure phrases (Satoshi Medium ~40px each), left-aligned below headline, one per line, full charcoal text. Isolated closing statement: Satoshi Bold ~60–70px, charcoal, positioned lower on card. Card brand line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A, Satoshi Medium ~24px, bottom center. Overall: typography-driven, clinical-warm, breathing whitespace, single green anchor, no overlays or illustration. Reusable for any topic: swap [headline], [list items], and [closing statement] while preserving layout, palette, and hierarchy.
posts.on_image: Headshot (clinical clinician, warm expression) | Headline: "Laughter is **regulatory**" (regulatory in green) | List: "Lowers cortisol | Shifts nervous system state | Releases built-up tension | Boosts immune signalling | Restores vagal tone" | Closing statement: "Joy is biology." | Brand line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition"
posts.caption: Play, joy and laughter aren't luxuries—they're measurable nervous-system regulators. The science is clear: when we laugh, cortisol drops, our vagal tone improves and our body moves out of fight-or-flight. Crying and connection do the same. If mental health feels stuck, sometimes the answer isn't another supplement—it's permission to play. What brings you genuine joy? Start there. Chat with us about building sustainable mental-health foundations. #MentalWellness #NervousSystem #FunctionalMedicine #AdelaideWellness #HolisticHealth
posts.gmb_caption: Joy and laughter are measurable nervous-system regulators, not optional extras. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Adelaide, we help you build sustainable mental-health foundations rooted in biology, not supplements alone. Ready to explore what real wellness feels like? Book an appointment today.

posts.visual_recipe: Talking-head style video still of a woman smiling warmly at camera in a clinical/office setting (blurred background). Bold teal rounded rectangle text overlay at lower third reading "The secret to getting kids to open up" in white sans-serif, medium-heavy weight font. Approximately 45-50% of frame height taken by overlay. Natural indoor lighting, warm and approachable tone.
posts.angle: Walking as a conversation starter — moving the body opens the mind.
posts.build_brief:
Format: Vertical reel cover (talking-head style, 9:16 Instagram Reels format).

Canvas: 1080 × 1920px on a soft cream background (#F5F3F0 or similar warm neutral).

Composition & layout (FAITHFUL to winner's structure):
- Talking-head shot: a warm, approachable EHN clinician or practitioner in a clinic/office setting with a gently blurred background (soft sage or cream tones, never harsh). Natural indoor lighting, direct-to-camera, gentle smile. Frame the person so they occupy the upper 50–55% of the canvas.
- Text overlay: a rounded-rectangle shape (EHN green #39B54A as the primary fill) positioned at the lower third of the frame, occupying approximately 40–45% of frame height. This is the visual anchor — prominent, warm, inviting.
- Text within the green rounded rectangle: headline in Satoshi Bold, white sans-serif, medium-heavy weight (~95–110px relative scale on 1080 canvas, no smaller than 95px on final render). Phrase: "Walk with someone today" or similar short, action-oriented phrase. Keep headline ≤6 words so text sits comfortably in the box without overcrowding.
- Subline (optional, if room): one short supporting phrase in Satoshi Medium, white, ~50–60px relative scale, e.g. "It opens more than doors." Keep it snappy.
- Whitespace: maintain breathing room inside the green box (padding ~40–60px all sides relative to 1080 canvas). The box should feel spacious, clinical, intentional — never cramped.
- Secondary accent (optional): a thin sage or muted teal line (~4–6px) or small icon (e.g. footsteps, a simple walking line-icon in white or soft cream) nestled in one corner of the green box, reinforcing movement without clutter.
- Legibility check: headline ≥95px, any secondary text ≥50px, all white on green #39B54A. No text below 36px.

Palette:
- Primary visual anchor: EHN green #39B54A (the rounded-rectangle fill).
- Background: soft cream (#F5F3F0) or warm white, maintaining a calm, clinical feel.
- Secondary accent (if used): sage green, soft teal, or muted warm grey — must harmonize with #39B54A, never clash.
- Type colour: white (headline + subline on green), charcoal or warm grey for any additional body text outside the box (if required).

Feel: warm, approachable, clinical-but-hopeful. The green-anchored box is the hero; the talking-head is trusted and relatable. No stock-wellness clichés — genuine, grounded, Australian-natural tone.

Branding: EHN logo (small, unobtrusive, ~40–60px) placed in top-left or bottom-left corner. No suburb name on the reel cover itself.
posts.design_recipe:
Format: Vertical reel cover (9:16 Instagram Reels format, 1080 × 1920px).

Canvas & background: soft cream or warm white, calm and clinical.

Layout (reusable structure):
- Upper 50–55% of frame: talking-head shot — a warm, approachable clinical practitioner, direct-to-camera, natural indoor lighting, blurred office/clinic background in soft neutral tones. Frame person from shoulders up.
- Lower section: rounded-rectangle shape filled with EHN green #39B54A, positioned at lower third, occupying ~40–45% of frame height. This is the visual anchor.
- Text hierarchy inside green box:
  - Headline: Satoshi Bold, white, ~95–110px relative scale (no smaller than 95px on final render), ≤6 words, short + action-oriented.
  - Subline (optional): Satoshi Medium, white, ~50–60px relative scale, one short phrase reinforcing the hook.
- Whitespace: ~40–60px padding inside the green box all sides. Spacious, intentional, breathing room.
- Secondary accent (optional): a thin sage or soft teal line (~4–6px) or minimalist icon (footsteps, line-icon, etc.) in one corner of the green box in white or soft cream — reinforces topic without clutter.

Legibility: headline ≥95px, secondary text ≥50px, all text ≥36px. Never shrink font; shorten copy instead. White type on green #39B54A for maximum contrast.

Colour anchor: EHN green #39B54A is the primary visual anchor (the rounded-rectangle). Secondary accents (if used) in sage, soft teal, or muted warm grey — must harmonize with green, never clash.

Branding: EHN logo (small, unobtrusive, ~40–60px) in top-left or bottom-left corner. No suburb name. Card close line "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (if displayed) in green #39B54A.

Feel: warm, clinical, grounded, Australian-natural. Talking-head builds trust; green anchor is the visual hero. Hopeful, conversational register.
posts.on_image:
Slide/frame (on-screen text overlay in green rounded rectangle at lower third):

Headline: "Walk with someone today"
Subline: "It opens more than doors."
posts.caption:
Walking isn't just a physical activity — it's one of the most powerful tools we have to connect, to talk, and to think clearly. When we move together, we lower the barriers. Conversations flow. Ideas surface. And our nervous systems settle.

If you've noticed your kids, partner, or friends open up more on a walk, you're not imagining it. There's real neuroscience behind it. Walking side-by-side (not face-to-face) takes the pressure off, and our brains do their best thinking when we're moving.

This week, invite someone for a walk. No agenda. Just movement and company.

What conversations happen for you on a walk? Drop us a line — we'd love to hear.

#WalkingForWellness #MovementMatters #MindBodyConnection #FunctionalMedicine #AdelaideMedicine
posts.gmb_caption: Walking is more than exercise — it's a conversation starter and a nervous-system reset. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we integrate movement into functional medicine because it works. Ready to explore how movement can support your whole health? Book an appointment today.

posts.visual_recipe: Professional headshot of a confident older male (66) in formal business attire (dark blazer, grey tie) speaking at a formal venue with leather chairs and dark curtains in background. Large white serif text overlaid on left side reads "I just turned 66. Here are 5 things I wish I knew at 26..." with "at 26" in italics. Approximately 25 words of large, bold, left-aligned text. Warm institutional setting conveys authority and wisdom. Dark background creates contrast for white text legibility.
posts.angle: 5 functional-medicine shifts I'd make earlier — what 40 years of practice taught me about ageing well.
posts.build_brief:
Create a single-slide carousel card (square, 1080 × 1080px) that mirrors the winner's layout exactly: professional headshot of a mature clinician (55–65) in natural clinical attire (soft green-toned cardigan or neutral linen blazer, calm expression) positioned centre-right, photographed in a warm, neutral clinical setting (soft cream or sage wall, natural window light, leather chair or wooden desk visible, no dark curtains — keep the institutional warmth but make it healing-aligned, not corporate).

On the LEFT side, layer large, bold, left-aligned Satoshi BOLD text (hierarchy ~95px headline + ~50px body, rendered as a tight block with tight leading). Headline reads: "I just turned 65. Here are 5 shifts I'd make earlier." with ONE meaningful word ("earlier" or "5") in green #39B54A italic. Below, add a short supporting line in Satoshi MEDIUM (~45px): "What 40 years of functional medicine taught me."

COLOUR PALETTE: Background cream or soft sage (#F5F8F6 or similar warm-neutral that harmonizes with #39B54A green — not pure white, not the competitor's dark palette). Green #39B54A must anchor the design: use it as the accent word, as a thin vertical rule or left border element, or as the background tint for the text block. Secondary colours: warm cream, charcoal for body copy. NO purple, orange, red, or neon.

WHITESPACE & COMPOSITION: Respect the winner's left-text, right-image split. Leave breathing room between text block and headshot. The headshot should feel warm, approachable, clinician-credible — NOT a corporate stock photo. The overall impression is "wisdom + healing," not "power."

FOOTER: Small EHN green text (Satoshi Medium, ~28px) in bottom right: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" with logo lockup (compact, unobtrusive).

TEXT ON CARD (see on_image for exact wording). Legibility floor: no text below 36px (headline ~95px, body ~45–50px, footer ~28px). If the supporting line feels cramped, shorten it rather than shrinking the font.
posts.design_recipe:
Reusable single-slide carousel template (square, 1080 × 1080px) anchored to EHN green #39B54A:

**Layout:** Left-text, right-image split (mirroring the personal-story, hopeful-register structure of the original).

**Background:** Warm neutral (cream, soft sage, or soft grey-blue) that harmonizes with #39B54A. Never pure white, never dark/charcoal dominant — the mood is warmth + healing.

**Text block (left side):**
- **Headline:** [meaningful phrase, ~8 words], Satoshi BOLD ~95px, left-aligned. ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic.
- **Supporting line:** [short subheading, ~2–3 words], Satoshi MEDIUM ~45–50px, left-aligned. Charcoal or neutral.
- Leave breathing room (whitespace) between headline and image. Tight leading (line-height ~1.2) within the headline itself.

**Image (right side):**
- Professional headshot or editorial photo (real person, warm clinical/natural setting, approachable expression, good window light). Centre-right placement. Photo should feel clinician-credible, not stock-wellness.
- OPTIONAL: thin vertical rule or left-border accent in green #39B54A to separate text from image.

**Visual anchors:**
- Green #39B54A as the italic accent word in headline (required).
- Secondary accent colour (soft sage, warm cream, or muted teal) used sparingly to enhance harmony — complement green, never clash.
- Charcoal or neutral text for body copy; ensure ≥40px for legibility.

**Footer (bottom right):**
- "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi MEDIUM, ~28px, green #39B54A or charcoal) + compact logo lockup (unobtrusive).

**Tone:** Clinical warmth, editorial polish, grounded wisdom. Whitespace-forward, breathing room between elements. No clutter, no busy backgrounds. The layout should feel as spacious and calm as the EHN brand.

**Legibility floors (hard):** Headline ≥95px, body ≥40px, footer ≥28px. If copy is long, shorten it — never shrink the font below these thresholds.
posts.on_image:
Headline: "I just turned 65. Here are 5 shifts I'd make *earlier*." (with "earlier" in green #39B54A italic)

Supporting line: "What 40 years of functional medicine taught me."

Footer: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green #39B54A)
posts.caption:
I just turned 65, and I've been reflecting on the choices that actually moved the dial — not the wellness noise, the real stuff.

If I could sit down with myself at 25, here's what I'd say: your body is a conversation, not a problem to fix. Sleep isn't a luxury. Stress is inflammatory. Your digestion is your foundation. And prevention beats intervention, every time.

These insights took me 40 years of clinical practice to land. But they're available to you right now — if you're willing to listen.

Ready to start? Book a consultation and let's talk about what your body needs.

#FunctionalMedicine #Longevity #Preventative #AustralianHealth #WellnessOver40
posts.gmb_caption: After 40 years in functional medicine, I've learned that true longevity starts with the foundations: sleep, stress, digestion, and prevention. If you're ready to take control of your health in Eastwood, Adelaide, let's work together. Book an appointment today.

posts.visual_recipe: Close-up bathroom photo of a person in a dark t-shirt standing in a dimly lit shower with warm orange candlelight and dried flowers visible on the ground. Text overlay in bold white sans-serif (medium-heavy weight, large size) positioned centre-frame reads "You were today years old when you found out you should be taking dark showers every night. Here's why.." The soft orange glow from candles creates an atmospheric, calming aesthetic against the neutral bathroom tiles and fixtures. The darkness and warm lighting reinforce the nervous-system-reset theme.
posts.angle: A simple nervous-system ritual to help you wind down and sleep deeper — using your shower as a reset tool.
posts.build_brief:
Format: vertical reel (1080×1920px, mobile-first).

Hook frame (first 1–2 seconds): Close-up, atmospheric photograph of a person in a loose neutral-toned garment standing in a dimly lit bathroom shower space. The lighting is warm and soft, created by small tealight candles and a scattering of dried botanicals (eucalyptus, native grasses) on the shower floor or ledge — very Adelaide-subtle, grounded in natural textures. The bathroom tiles are warm grey or soft sage; fixtures are brushed metal or ceramic. NO orange glow — instead, use a soft, cool-to-warm transition (cream-to-sage) that harmonises with EHN green #39B54A. The overall mood is calm, clinical, and restorative — not a spa cliché.

On-screen text (centred, upper-middle frame, large hierarchical treatment):
— Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95px+ on 1080 canvas, positioned centre-frame with breathing room): "You were today years old when you found out" (first line, white or off-white text, strong weight)
— Supporting line (Satoshi Bold, one word in green #39B54A italic — ideally "showers" or "reset" — ~60–70px relative scale): "[one word in green #39B54A italic]" continues the sentence to "…you should be taking cool showers every night."
— Subline (Satoshi Medium, ~40–45px, cream or very light grey, positioned below with gentle whitespace): "Here's why." (optional beat, or fold into the line above)

The text occupies the centre-vertical plane without crowding; whitespace mirrors the winner's layout (generous top, breathing room around text). Background colour: warm soft grey (#f5f5f5) or a very subtle sage tint (#f8f9f7) that lets the green anchor and warm bathroom tones sit comfortably. Never pure black or the competitor's orange palette.

Secondary visual anchor: small EHN logo (small, unobtrusive, bottom-right corner, 40–50px, no background).

Palette: EHN green #39B54A as the primary anchor (the one highlighted word), warm cream, soft grey, sage undertones, brushed metal accents from fixtures. All colours harmonise; nothing clashes with green.

Legibility lock: all text >=36px (headline ~95px+, subline ~40px minimum). No text should render below 36px; shorten rather than shrink.

Composition mirrors the winner exactly: atmospheric close-up (person + bathroom detail), text centred and overlaid, warm-but-clinical aesthetic, nervous-system-reset register (hope, ritual, simplicity).
posts.design_recipe:
Format: vertical reel (1080×1920px, mobile-first).

Reusable layout shell:
— Hook frame: atmospheric close-up photograph (person + environment detail related to the topic) in warm, muted tones, shot in natural or soft accent lighting. Avoid pure black or neon palettes.
— Background colour: warm soft grey, cream, or a muted complementary tint (e.g. sage for nervous-system topics, soft blue-grey for clarity topics) that harmonises with EHN green #39B54A and never clashes.
— On-screen text (centred, upper-middle frame, large hierarchical treatment):
  · Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95px+ relative scale on 1080 canvas): "[hook question or statement]" (white or off-white, positioned centre-frame with breathing room)
  · Supporting line (Satoshi Bold, one meaningful word in green #39B54A italic, ~60–70px relative): "[one word in green #39B54A italic] [continuation of headline]"
  · Subline (Satoshi Medium, ~40–45px, cream or light neutral, positioned below with gentle whitespace): "[short supporting beat or question]"
— Whitespace: generous top margin, breathing room around all text, no crowding. Centre-vertical text plane.
— Logo: small EHN lockup, bottom-right corner (40–50px, unobtrusive).
— Palette: EHN green #39B54A as primary anchor (the highlighted word); secondary colours chosen to harmonise with green and suit the topic/mood (sage, soft grey-blue, warm cream, muted terracotta, soft neutrals). No purple, bright pink, orange, strong red, neon. All colours intentional and complementary.
— Mood: atmospheric, clinical-but-warm, grounded, editorial — never stock wellness cliché.

This shell can be grafted onto any topic (sleep, digestion, hormones, energy, stress, immunity) by replacing the photograph subject/environment, the text placeholders, and the secondary colour harmonic while keeping the green anchor, the centred text hierarchy, the whitespace ratio, and the reel format constant.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1 (hook frame):
Headline line 1: "You were today years old when you found out"
Headline line 2: "cool [showers] [resets] your nervous system every night."
Subline: "Here's why."

(All text Satoshi: headline Bold, subline Medium; "showers" or "resets" in green #39B54A italic; positioned centre-frame with breathing room.)
posts.caption:
You know that wired-but-tired feeling? The one where your mind won't switch off even though your body's exhausted?

Cool showers are one of our favourite low-cost nervous-system resets — especially in the evening. A brief dip below body temperature (think 15–20°C, not ice-bath cold) triggers your parasympathetic nervous system, helping you move from "fight or flight" into "rest and digest" mode.

The ritual itself matters too: dim the lights, skip the hairdryer buzz, and just *be* in the water for a few minutes. Your skin receptors are doing the heavy lifting.

Give it a go for a week and notice what shifts in your sleep quality. Most patients report easier wind-down within 3–5 nights.

Curious about your own nervous-system patterns? Let's chat about what's blocking your sleep.

#SleepSupport #NervousSystemHealth #FunctionalMedicine #AdelaideWellness #SlowLiving
posts.gmb_caption: Struggling to wind down at night? Cool showers are a simple, evidence-backed ritual to calm your nervous system and improve sleep — even here in warm Adelaide. Book a consultation to explore your sleep patterns and get personalised strategies that work for your body.

posts.visual_recipe: Dark teal/navy solid background with a focused professional headshot of a middle-aged man wearing glasses in the lower half. Bold white sans-serif headline text (approx 60pt) reads "HOW I REVERSED MY BIOLOGICAL AGE" in upper third, with the key metric "FROM 52 TO 42" rendered in bright red (approx 48pt) below. Man holds a supplement bottle toward camera (slight out-of-focus). Warm amber ambient lighting in background (blurred bookshelf). High contrast, authority-signaling composition that blends personal credibility (the face, the credentials implied) with curiosity-driving numbers.
posts.angle: How functional-medicine testing revealed the methylation block nobody else had caught—and what we did about it
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: single square card (1080 × 1080px).

Layout: Replicate the winner's hierarchy and whitespace exactly.
• Upper third: Headline in Satoshi Bold, approx 95px scale, white text on a soft sage-grey-blue background (a muted, cooling neutral that harmonizes with EHN green #39B54A). The headline reads "METHYLATION BLOCKS NOBODY SAW" with the word "METHYLATION" in EHN green #39B54A, italic, bold.
• Middle third: A supporting stat or insight in Satoshi Medium, white text, approx 50–60px scale (readable hierarchy, shorter than headline). Example: "UNTIL WE DID". Keep it punchy and curious.
• Lower half: A professional, clinical headshot of a patient (or clinician) holding or pointing to a functional-medicine test result (lab printout, biomarker chart, or wellness device). The figure is positioned in the lower half, slightly frame-right, mirroring the winner's composition. Warm, ambient, professional lighting. Out-of-focus warm cream or soft amber bookshelf/clinic background to signal authority and care.
• Visual anchor: A vertical stripe or accent line in EHN green #39B54A running down the left edge or right edge of the card (20–30px wide, full height or partial), anchoring the design in green without overwhelming it.
• Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium, white text on a small green #39B54A footer bar or badge at the foot of the card (approx 28–32px scale, legible but subordinate).
• Composition: High contrast between the cool sage background and the warm ambient lighting in the photo. Authority-signaling, clinical credibility, personal warmth. No pure black, no competitor's navy or teal. No stock imagery—real person, real test, real clinic energy.
• Finish: Uncluttered, hierarchical, clinician-led trust-building. The green stripe anchors; the headline hook draws; the face and test result ground the promise in lived reality.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: single square card (1080 × 1080px, 1:1 ratio).

Reusable structure (topic-agnostic template):
• Background: Soft sage-grey-blue (or complementary muted neutral that harmonizes with EHN green #39B54A, never pure black, never bright or clashing secondary). This cooling background supports any functional-medicine or wellness topic.
• Layout zones:
  - Upper third (40% vertical): Headline zone. Satoshi Bold, ~95px relative scale. White/light text on the sage background. One meaningful word highlighted in EHN green #39B54A italic. Copy short (max 5–6 words) to preserve legibility and impact.
  - Middle third (20% vertical): Supporting insight or stat. Satoshi Medium, ~50–60px relative scale. White/light text. Short phrase, punchy tone (max 3 words). Builds curiosity.
  - Lower half (40% vertical): Visual anchor zone. Professional headshot (real person, real clinical setting, real object—test result, lab slip, device, supplement, measurement tool) positioned frame-lower-right or frame-lower-left. Warm, ambient lighting (soft gold/cream background, blurred bookshelf, clinic environment). The figure holds or references the object toward camera. High contrast with background.
• Green anchor (REQUIRED): Vertical accent stripe (20–30px wide, EHN green #39B54A) running edge-to-edge or partial-height down one side of the card. Alternatively, a small green badge, footer bar, or accent shape integrating the close line. Green must be prominent but not overwhelming—visual anchor, not wallpaper.
• Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium, white text, ~28–32px relative scale. Nestled in a small green footer bar, badge, or accent at the bottom of the card. Subordinate to headline and image, but present and branded.
• Typography lock: All text Satoshi (Satoshi Bold for headline, Satoshi Medium for supporting text and close line). No other fonts. Maintain consistent weight hierarchy.
• Legibility floors (HARD): Headline ≥95px relative scale; supporting text ≥50–60px relative scale; body/close line ≥28–32px relative scale. No text below 36px on a 1080 canvas. If copy is longer, shorten it; never shrink the font.
• Palette flexibility: The sage-grey-blue background can shift to complement the topic—soft cream, warm grey, charcoal, or muted teal—but must always harmonize with EHN green #39B54A and NEVER clash (no bright secondary colours, no pure black, no competitor's exact palette).
• Composition: High-contrast, authority-signaling, personal-credibility-driven. The face and object ground the promise; the numbers/insight spark curiosity; the green stripe anchors the brand. Clinical warmth, not salesy. Editorial, grounded, real.
• Finish: Uncluttered, hierarchical, clinician-led. The same template can host any functional-medicine topic (methylation, detox, inflammation, hormones, microbiome, etc.) by swapping headline, stat, image, and supporting text while preserving the layout, green anchor, and authority tone.

posts.caption:
Methylation underpins everything from energy and mood to how your body handles stress and detoxifies. When it stalls, standard pathology often misses it—the markers sit "in range" but the functional picture is quietly breaking down.

This patient's methylation cycle was blocked at a critical gate. Their methylated B12 was low. Their homocysteine looked "fine" on paper. Their energy, focus, and recovery were not.

We rebalanced cofactors, supported the cycle, and watched the fog lift.

If you're tired, foggy, or stuck despite "normal" bloods, methylation might be the conversation we need to have.

Book a functional consultation to dig deeper.

#Methylation #FunctionalMedicine #B12 #Homocysteine #EnergyRestoration #Adelaide
posts.gmb_caption: Methylation blocks often hide in "normal" lab ranges—but the tiredness, brain fog, and slow recovery are real. Our functional-medicine testing in Eastwood, Adelaide catches what standard pathology misses and maps a clear path to restoration. Book an appointment to explore what's really driving your fatigue.

posts.visual_recipe: Newspaper feature (Times Saturday edition) with professional headshot of male doctor in white coat with stethoscope, positioned right of frame. Large serif headline "Drug-free doctor: The GP who..." in dark text on white background. Pull-quote in blue overlay box on photo reads "The number of prescriptions we give out as doctors has increased by over 80 per cent in the past 20 years" - medium serif font. Body text in two columns left side. Grey concrete background visible at top. High-authority editorial aesthetic.
posts.angle: Functional medicine doctor challenges the normalisation of prescriptions—and what it means for long-term health
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: 1080px square carousel (4 slides, or adapt to 2–3 if copy tightens).

Slide 1 — Title card:
- Background: Warm cream (#F5F1ED or similar) with subtle charcoal accent bar (20px) top-left corner for visual anchor.
- Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95–100px, centre-left): "Rethink" in green #39B54A italic, "prescriptions" in charcoal below.
- Subline (Satoshi Medium, ~50px, charcoal): "What functional medicine asks instead"
- Green vertical rule (8–12px wide, #39B54A) left edge, running 60% of card height — mimics the newspaper's editorial grid.
- Logo (small, unobtrusive, bottom-right corner, 80–100px).
- Whitespace-heavy, editorial feel — no busy photography yet.

Slide 2 — Authority pull-quote + figure:
- Background: Soft sage-grey (#E8EDE8 or warm neutral that harmonises with green).
- Left side (60%): Small professional portrait of clinician (or generic clinical illustration in muted teal/sage, grounded style — NO stock wellness clichés) in a subtle 4px border in green #39B54A.
- Right side (40%): Pull-quote in large Satoshi Medium (~48–52px, charcoal) on a soft teal or muted blue-green overlay box (#A8C5B8 or similar — complementary to EHN green, NOT the competitor's blue).
- Quote text: "Prescriptions per patient have doubled in the past 20 years. Functional medicine asks: why is this happening?" (shortened, EHN-voice, no stat inflation).
- Green accent line (4–6px) beneath quote box.

Slide 3 — Two-column evidence point:
- Background: Cream or off-white, matching Slide 1.
- Left column (Satoshi Medium, ~44px, charcoal): "What's changed?" with 3–4 short bullet points (e.g., "Symptom management over root cause" / "Shorter consultation times" / "Marketing of pharmaceuticals").
- Right column (Satoshi Medium, ~44px, charcoal): "What we do differently" with parallel points in functional-medicine language (e.g., "Investigate underlying imbalance" / "Time to listen" / "Evidence-based supplementation where needed").
- Small green divider line (2–3px, #39B54A) between columns, echoing newspaper layout.
- Whitespace between points — NOT dense.

Slide 4 — Call-to-action close:
- Background: Charcoal (#2C2C2C) or deep warm grey, NOT pure black.
- Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~70px, cream/off-white): "Let's talk about" in cream, then "your health story" in green #39B54A italic.
- Body (Satoshi Medium, ~44px, cream): "Book a consultation to explore what functional medicine can do for you."
- Green button/highlight area (subtle, #39B54A) framing the CTA.
- "Elemental Health & Nutrition" brand lockup (cream text, small, bottom-right).
- Logo (cream, small, bottom-left).

OVERALL COMPOSITION:
- Mimic the newspaper feature: large, clear hierarchy; one meaningful serif-style weight (use Satoshi Bold for headlines); two-column body layout where relevant; pull-quote as a hero moment; professional authority aesthetic.
- Whitespace ratio: 20–25% of each slide should be breathing room (never crammed).
- Green #39B54A as the visual anchor on every slide: headline italic, divider lines, accent boxes, button highlight, borders.
- Secondary palette: soft sage, teal, warm cream, charcoal — all chosen to harmonise with green, never clash.
- Typography scale: headlines ~95–100px, subheads ~50px, body ~44px, pull-quote ~48–52px. NO text below 36px.
- Clinical-but-warm: editorial tone, professional credibility, grounded colour world (no neon, no cliché wellness imagery).
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: 1080px square carousel (4 slides, scalable to 2–3 if content compresses).

REUSABLE TEMPLATE:

Slide 1 — Anchor card (Title):
- Background: Warm cream or off-white.
- Charcoal accent bar (20px) top-left corner.
- Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95–100px): [first word or key noun] in green #39B54A italic, [second/supporting word] in charcoal below, centre-left alignment.
- Subline (Satoshi Medium, ~50px, charcoal): [thematic descriptor or promise].
- Green vertical rule (8–12px, #39B54A) left edge, 60% of card height.
- Logo (small, unobtrusive, bottom-right).
- Whitespace-heavy, editorial feel.

Slide 2 — Authority moment (Portrait + pull-quote):
- Background: Soft sage-grey or warm neutral harmonising with green.
- Left (60%): Small professional portrait (4px green border, #39B54A) or grounded clinical illustration in muted teal/sage.
- Right (40%): Pull-quote in large Satoshi Medium (~48–52px, charcoal) on subtle complementary overlay box (e.g., muted teal #A8C5B8 or sage, NOT competitor's palette).
- Green accent line (4–6px) beneath quote box.

Slide 3 — Evidence/comparison (Two-column layout):
- Background: Cream or off-white.
- Left column (Satoshi Medium, ~44px, charcoal): [category heading] with 3–4 short bullet points.
- Right column (Satoshi Medium, ~44px, charcoal): [parallel category heading] with corresponding bullet points.
- Green divider line (2–3px, #39B54A) between columns.
- Breathing room between points (never dense).

Slide 4 — Close card (CTA):
- Background: Charcoal (#2C2C2C) or deep warm grey, NOT pure black.
- Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~70px, cream): [opening phrase] in cream, [call-to-action word] in green #39B54A italic.
- Body (Satoshi Medium, ~44px, cream): [invitation copy].
- Green highlight/button area (#39B54A) subtly framing CTA.
- "Elemental Health & Nutrition" brand lockup (cream, small, bottom-right).
- Logo (cream, small, bottom-left).

PALETTE:
- Green anchor: #39B54A (headlines italic, dividers, borders, accents, button).
- Secondary colours: soft sage, muted teal, warm cream, charcoal — chosen per topic to harmonise with green, never clash.
- Backgrounds: cream, off-white, charcoal. NO pure black, NO competitor's exact palette.

HIERARCHY & WHITESPACE:
- Headline ~95–100px (Satoshi Bold), subhead ~50px (Satoshi Medium), pull-quote ~48–52px (Satoshi Medium), body ~44px (Satoshi Medium).
- NO text below 36px.
- 20–25% whitespace per slide; text never crammed.
- Two-column body layout (Slide 3) echoes newspaper editorial structure.

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Headings: Satoshi Bold (700).
- Body/subline: Satoshi Medium (500).
- One meaningful word per headline highlighted in green #39B54A italic (or complementary accent if design requires).

BRAND & COMPLIANCE:
- Card close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (never suburb).
- Logo: small, unobtrusive (bottom-left or bottom-right, consistent position).
- Voice: clinical-credible, warm, plain-English, Australian spelling, evidence-based. NO cure/treat/guarantee/miracle claims, NO supplement pitches, NO before-after imagery.
- This template scaffolds any functional-medicine topic while maintaining editorial authority and green-anchored brand presence.

posts.caption:
We love a Times feature as much as anyone—but prescription rates tell only part of the story.

In functional medicine, we start by asking *why*. Why has one patient's fatigue persisted despite multiple medications? Why does another person's blood sugar remain unsettled? The answers almost always point to something deeper: an imbalance in nutrition, sleep, stress, or digestion that conventional prescribing alone can't address.

Don't misunderstand—pharmaceuticals save lives. But when we treat symptoms without investigating root cause, we often trade one problem for another. Our job is to do the harder work: listen, test, think, and build a plan that gets to the heart of it.

Ready to explore a different approach to your health? Let's chat.

#FunctionalMedicine #RootCause #Nutrition #Wellness #AdelaideClinician
posts.gmb_caption: Functional medicine in Eastwood, Adelaide asks a different question: not just "what should we treat?", but "why is this happening?" Book a consultation to explore how we investigate root cause and build a health plan that actually sticks.

posts.visual_recipe: Text-only Facebook post with no image available. Body structured as 4 myth-busting bullet points (kibble renderers, spaying timing, neurotoxin flea/tick meds, vaccine marketing), each debunking conventional pet care. Closes with podcast CTA. The copy itself is the visual delivery—designed for scroll-stopping, scannable format with fear/authority blend to drive engagement and link clicks."
posts.angle: Mould, microplastics and daily toxin exposure: what functional medicine can actually address.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: single text-only card (square 1080 x 1080px, or vertical for feed flexibility). Background: soft warm cream (#F5F3F0) — a neutral that harmonizes gently with EHN green without overwhelming. 

Layout: Myth-bust structure, scannable and dense. Opens with a contrarian hook in large headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95–110px), one meaningful word in green #39B54A italic (e.g., "told"). Hook sits in the upper third with breathing room above (white space ~60–80px top margin). 

Below the hook, insert a short alarming stat or statement (Satoshi Medium, ~48–56px, charcoal #2C2C2C) — 1–2 lines, establishing urgency. Whitespace buffer (~40px) below.

Body: 4 myth-busting bullet points (Satoshi Medium, 40–44px per line, charcoal). Each bullet is a short phrase (6–10 words max, never wrapping beyond 2 lines). Bullets sit left-aligned with a small green #39B54A circle or dash preceding each. Spacing between bullets: ~32–40px. The bullet points are the visual anchor — they're scannable, breathable, not dense.

Close: 1–2 lines (Satoshi Medium, 40px) soft CTA or clinician signature (e.g., "Ready to explore a clearer path? Book a functional medicine consult."). 

Bottom bar: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Bold, ~32–36px, green #39B54A on cream background). Small EHN logo lockup sits to the left of the text, unobtrusive.

Colour hierarchy: Cream background, green #39B54A as the only accent (headline word, bullets, close line), charcoal body text. No secondary colours—let green be the sole hero. This mirrors the winner's fear/authority register but softens it with EHN's clinical warmth and Australian grounded palette.
posts.design_recipe:
Reusable myth-bust template (text-only card, EHN green-anchored):

Canvas: Square card (1080 x 1080px). Background: soft warm cream (#F5F3F0), harmonizing with but distinct from EHN green.

Composition: Upper third reserved for headline + whitespace. Headline in Satoshi Bold (~95–110px), one meaningful word in green #39B54A italic. Upper margin ~60–80px for breathing room.

Middle section: short stat or statement (Satoshi Medium, ~48–56px, charcoal), 1–2 lines, establishing register (urgency, authority, myth-bust tone). ~40px whitespace below.

Body layer: 4 scannable bullet points (Satoshi Medium, 40–44px per line). Each line max 10 words, 2-line max per bullet. Green #39B54A circle or dash precedes each point. Left-aligned, ~32–40px spacing between bullets. This is the visual anchor—dense but breathable.

Close: 1–2 lines (Satoshi Medium, 40px), soft CTA or clinician voice. ~40px whitespace above.

Bottom bar: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Bold, ~32–36px, green #39B54A). Small EHN logo lockup left of text. Unobtrusive, brand-locked.

Colour lock: Cream background, green #39B54A as sole accent (headline, bullets, close, brand line), charcoal body. No secondary colours. Green anchors every scan-stop element.

posts.caption: Anxiety doesn't have to hijack your day. When panic or worry starts to spiral, there are four grounded steps your nervous system actually responds to — no willpower needed, no supplements required. We walk through them here: slow your breath to signal safety, ground yourself in the present moment, and gently move your body. Most people feel the shift within minutes. If anxiety is a regular visitor, a functional-medicine check-in can help us find what's driving it — food sensitivities, sleep patterns, stress load, or something else entirely. Worth exploring. Ready to get to the root? Book a consultation with our team in Eastwood. #AnxietyRelief #NervousSystemHealth #FunctionalMedicine #MentalWellnessAU #ElemtnalHealthAdelaide
posts.gmb_caption: Anxiety spiralling? Here are four simple steps to settle your nervous system in the moment — no willpower required. Watch how to move through panic with grounded, evidence-based techniques. If anxiety is a regular pattern, our team in Eastwood can help find what's driving it. Book a consultation today.

posts.visual_recipe: Minimalist text-only card on white background with left-aligned black sans-serif text in varying font sizes/weights. Opens with a bold statement (~12 words), followed by a listicle of ~10 short actionable practices (each 3-5 words, one per line), then two closing paragraphs explaining the mechanism. Author byline and handle at bottom in smaller type. Clean, spacious layout with ample negative space between sections.
posts.angle: How chronic stress rewires your hormones—and what stable nervous system actually feels like.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: single square card (1080×1080). Background: soft cream (#F5F3F0) with ample negative space—echo the winner's minimalist, breathing layout. 

Headline section (top third): Left-align a bold statement in Satoshi Bold (~95px, ample leading). Colour the headline in charcoal (#2C2C2C); isolate ONE meaningful word (e.g., "Stress") and colour it green #39B54A italic to anchor the card and create visual hierarchy. 

Body listicle (middle, ~50% of card): Six short, actionable lines (each 3–5 words, one per line), left-aligned in Satoshi Medium (~48px), charcoal text. Separate each line with breathing space—match the winner's density and whitespace rhythm. No bullets; let the typography carry the rhythm.

Closing paragraph (lower third): Two short closing sentences in Satoshi Medium (~40px) explaining the mechanism—plain language, clinical tone, left-aligned. Maintain the spacious feel; do not crowd.

Byline section (bottom): Small Satoshi Medium (~32px, charcoal) name and credential (e.g., "Sarah, Functional Medicine Practitioner"). 

Brand close line (footer): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Bold (~28px), green #39B54A, left-aligned.

Overall: Respect the winner's whitespace-to-text ratio and left-aligned vertical flow. Never use pure black, pure white, or the competitor's exact palette. Green #39B54A is the only colour accent; charcoal and cream are the neutral anchors. Clean, spacious, clinical-but-warm—an editorial feel, never stock wellness.
posts.design_recipe:
**Reusable EHN Minimalist Text-Only Card Template**

Canvas: Single square card (1080×1080). 

Background: Soft cream (#F5F3F0)—a warm neutral that grounds green #39B54A and invites breathing space. Never pure white, never pure black, never a full green background.

**Layout & Hierarchy:**
- **Headline section** (top ~25%): Left-aligned bold statement in Satoshi Bold (~95px relative scale). One meaningful word highlighted in green #39B54A italic; remaining headline in charcoal (#2C2C2C).
- **Listicle body** (middle ~50%): 6–8 short lines (3–5 words each, one per line) in Satoshi Medium (~48px relative scale), charcoal, left-aligned. Generous vertical breathing between lines—mimic the winner's spacious rhythm.
- **Closing mechanism** (lower ~15%): Two short sentences in Satoshi Medium (~40px relative scale), charcoal, left-aligned. Plain-language explanation of the why.
- **Byline** (bottom ~5%): Small Satoshi Medium (~32px relative scale), charcoal, name + credential.
- **Brand close** (footer): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Bold (~28px relative scale), green #39B54A, left-aligned.

**Colour Palette:**
- Primary visual anchor: green #39B54A (one headline word, brand close line).
- Neutrals: charcoal (#2C2C2C) for all body and byline text; cream (#F5F3F0) for background.
- No secondary colours; green is the only accent. Harmony through restraint and whitespace.

**Overall Feel:** Minimalist, editorial, clinical-but-warm. Ample negative space. Vertical left-aligned flow. Satoshi throughout (Bold for headlines, Medium for body). No imagery, no icons—typography and breathing space do the work. AHPRA/TGA-safe, no cure/treat/guarantee claims.
posts.on_image:
Headline: "How **Stress** Rewires Your Hormones"

Listicle:
Calm your nervous system first
Breathe deeply, daily
Move your body gently
Sleep in a dark room
Eat whole foods, regularly
Connect with people you trust

Closing:
When your nervous system feels unsafe, stress hormones rise—cortisol, adrenaline. Over time, this shifts your menstrual cycle, mood, and energy. Stability returns when you signal safety to your brain.

Byline: Sarah, Functional Medicine Practitioner

Brand: Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Perimenopause doesn't happen in a vacuum—it's deeply wired to how safe your nervous system feels.

When stress is chronic, your brain keeps stress hormones elevated. Over months or years, this can shift your cycle, flatten your mood, and drain your energy. The good news? Your nervous system is plastic. When you signal safety—through breath, sleep, movement, and connection—your hormones tend to settle.

This isn't about perfection or one "fix." It's about meeting your body's need for calm, consistently, in ways that feel real to your life.

If your hormones feel chaotic right now, that's worth exploring with a clinician who understands the nervous-system link.

Ready to get your hormones back on side? Let's chat.

#Perimenopause #HormoneHealth #NervousSystem #FunctionalMedicine #AdelaideHealth
posts.gmb_caption: Perimenopause and stress are deeply connected. When your nervous system feels unsafe, stress hormones rise and can throw your cycle off balance. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we help you understand this link and build lasting calm. Book an appointment with us to explore your hormonal health today.
